items.title: How to update Craft CMS/plugins without downtime?
items.body: On our production server, we have strict SLAs concerning when our site can be down. I've noticed that when I update Craft CMS 3 (using "./craft update all" on the server), the site will go down for anywhere from 1-30 seconds. Once the update is completed, everything returns to working. Has anyone seen this before? How can I update Craft without downtime?

items.title: Multi-Site Craft 3 - How to set defaultTokenDuration to shorter duration for just 1 of the sites?
items.body:
I currently have a multi-site Craft 3 install with this in the general.php config file (amongst other settings)

return [
   '*' => [
       'defaultTokenDuration' => 259200, //72 hours
    ]
]

Which works fine for all sites allowing logged in users to remain logged in for 72 hours. I'm adding a new site where we need the duration to be 30 minutes.

I tried this:

return [
   '*' => [
        'defaultTokenDuration' => [
            '*' => 259200, //72 hours
            'otherSite' => 1800 //30 minutes
        ]
    ]
]

where 'otherSite' is the handle of the new site. Craft is throwing this error:

yii\base\InvalidConfigException: Unable to convert to seconds. in /Users/chad/Repos/gcx-craft/vendor/craftcms/cms/src/helpers/ConfigHelper.php:49

I also tried replacing the * with the handle of the primary site and saw the same error, so I assume listing all sites with a value in that array is useless. Is there any way to specify different values for different sites for this setting?

items.title: Possible to limit current available asset sources by current active site?
items.body:
I have a multi-site setup and I'd ideally only like the author to be able to upload an asset to the asset source associated with the currently active site. For example, I have an author that has access to three sites, but I'd like to remove the risk of someone uploading a Site A image to the Site B asset directory by mistake by only displaying the relevant source in the sidebar.

However, due to how many different sites we'll probably end up with, it won't be scalable for me to create a field that targets each source for each site-usergroup combo for each site.

Would there be a logical way for a module to identify the current site and hide all sources that aren't associated with it?

items.title: Updating private repo plugins from admin?
items.body: Is it possible to update private repo plugins from the admin at all? Would love that functionality, especially for smaller projects that are installed where there's no CLI access.

items.title: Can I use an Azure DevOps (ADO) repo with Craft Cloud?
items.body:
We are looking at Craft Cloud for our new web site/application. Our company has standardized on Azure DevOps (ADO) as our platform of choice. Cloud only lists Github, Gitlab and Bitbucket as git repo options; there is no “custom” or ADO option.

Is there anyway to work around this, and utilize ADO?

items.title: Setting up a new Next.js app with Craft
items.body:
I'm having trouble fetching data in a new Next.js app from Craft.

  • I'm on Next 15.0.3 and Craft 5.5.

  • I've got CRAFT_CMS_GRAPHQL_TOKEN and CRAFT_CMS_GRAPHQL_ENDPOINT set up in my .env file.

  • I've got 'api' => 'graphql/api' in routes.php.

  • I've created my schema in Craft and have tested it with graphiql.

  • I've got a fetchApi.tsx that looks like this:

    export async function fetchData(graphql) {
let craftUrl = process.env.CRAFT_CMS_GRAPHQL_ENDPOINT;

const res = await fetch(craftUrl, {
  method: 'post',
  body: graphql,
  headers: {
    'Content-Type': 'application/graphql',
    'Authorization': `Bearer ${process.env.CRAFT_CMS_GRAPHQL_TOKEN}`
  },
});

if (res.status !== 200) {
  throw new Error('Failed to fetch API');
}

const json = await res.json();

if (json.errors) {
  console.error(json.errors);
  throw new Error('Failed to fetch API');
}

return json.data;

    }

and then in page.tsx I have

import { fetchData } from "./utils/fetchApi";

async function getContent() {
  const data = await fetchData(`
    {
      entries(section: "content") {
        id
        ... on content_Entry {
          id
          title
        }
      }
    }
  `);

  return data.content;
}

export default async function RootLayout({ children }) {
  const content = await getContent();

  console.log(content);
}

With that I get

[ Server ] Error: fetch failed

pointing at const res = await fetch(craftUrl….

Some Googling suggested using localhost or 127.0.0.1 instead of my dev URL and I've tried both of those but get the same result.

I can hit my API URL in a browser and it returns a message:

No GraphQL query was supplied

so it seems that it's only a problem accessing it from within Next. Anyone got any advice on what's missing from my set-up?

items.title: /cpresources in a cloud storage
items.body: Is it possible to have /cpresources in a cloud storage like Azure Blob storage and that Craft writes them to the cloud when needed? Thanks!

items.title: How can I make CKEditor use a default protocol if one is not entered in the link field?
items.body:
When adding a link in the CKEditor text block the content editors of the website frequently just put 'www.website.com' instead of including 'http://' or 'https://' before it. Is there a way to set CKEditor to default to HTTP:// if no protocol is included, or add a dropdown for the protocol so it's automatically selected but can be changed.

When a protocol is not included in the link they input, it comes out on the frontend like 'https://mydomain.com/website.com', for example.

I'm using Craft4 Pro and CKEditor on most recent versions

items.title: The YAML value does not appear to be valid UTF-8
items.body:
Today it took me hours to solve a weird problem, so I want to share this:

  • I created an easy-going local DDEV Craft 5 instance on my mac.
  • I packed the project into a tar.gz archive
  • I transferred it manually to my test server
  • After my initial log-in into the CP I always received this exception: "The YAML value does not appear to be valid UTF-8"

items.title: How to determine if an entry is new or a published entry on the entry edit page
items.body:
I want a lightswitch to default to on if the entry is new. I originally checked for whether or not $entry->postDate is null, but postDate is already defined set to what seems like the timestamp from when I clicked New entry in the Entries-menu. Is this even the intended behavior?

What can I check to determine if this is an existing (published) entry or a new, unpublished entry? Both isDraft are true, but only one of them comes from an existing entry while the other case it is brand new.

Event::on(
    Entry::class,
    Element::EVENT_DEFINE_SIDEBAR_HTML,
    function (DefineHtmlEvent $event) {
        /** @var Entry $entry */
        $entry = $event->sender;
        
        $isNewEntry = $entry->postDate == null; // <-- TODO: Insert logic here
        
        $lightSwitchHtml = Cp::lightswitchFieldHtml([
            'label' => 'My Lightswitch',
            'id' => 'myLightswitch',
            'name' => 'myLightswitch',
            'on' => $isNewEntry,
        ]);

        $event->html .= $lightSwitchHtml;
    }
);

But registering users via a front end form wil allow one to enter an email that is already being used. Several times.

How is that possible?

All of these ar TRUE:

Verify email addresses
Allow public registration
Validate custom fields on public registration
Deactivate users by default

Craft 5 Pro etc.

I'm trying to get some feedback for the user on registration when the username already exists but I don't know in which object the errors are.

I use the form they provide in the docs: https://docs.craftcms.com/v3/dev/examples/user-registration-form.html

Same for the forgot password form if the user doesn't exists in the system, I'm not able the provide the right feedback with this form: https://docs.craftcms.com/v3/dev/examples/forgot-password-form.html

items.title: Postgres SSL PDO config for config/db.php
items.body:
Struggling to configure this correctly and cleanly. When I upgrade to latest 3.x Craft on staging, Craft can no longer connect to the Postgres DB, which uses SSL. Have had success up until CraftCMS version 3.7.28 by overwriting vendor/craftcms/cms/src/config/DbConfig.php and with a config/db.php as follows:

<?php
return [
    'dsn' => 'pgsql:host=' . getenv('DB_SERVER') .
         ';port=' . getenv('DB_PORT') . 
         ';dbname=' . getenv('DB_DATABASE') .
         ';sslmode=require' . 
         ';sslcert=/etc/ssl/certs/ssl-cert-pg.pem' . 
         ';sslkey=/etc/ssl/private/ssl-cert-pg.key' .
         ';sslrootcert=/etc/ssl/certs/ssl-cert-pg.pem' .
         ';user=' . getenv('DB_USER') .
         ';password=' . getenv('DB_PASSWORD'),
    'attributes' => []
];

The vendor/craftcms/cms/src/config/DbConfig.php override that functioned previously is summarized here:

public function init()
{
    if ($this->dsn) {
        if (($pos = strpos($this->dsn, ':')) === false) {
            throw new InvalidConfigException('Invalid DSN: ' . $this->dsn);
        }
        $this->driver = substr($this->dsn, 0, $pos);
        $params = substr($this->dsn, $pos + 1);
        foreach (explode(';', $params) as $param) {
            if (($pos = strpos($param, '=')) === false) {
                throw new InvalidConfigException('Invalid DSN param: ' . $param . 'in - params: ' . $params);
            }
            $paramName = substr($param, 0, $pos);
            $paramValue = substr($param, $pos + 1);
            switch ($paramName) {
                case 'host':
                    $this->server = $paramValue;
                    break;
                case 'port':
                    $this->port = $paramValue;
                    break;
                case 'dbname':
                    $this->database = $paramValue;
                    break;
                case 'unix_socket':
                    $this->unixSocket = $paramValue;
                    break;
                case 'charset':
                    $this->charset = $paramValue;
                    break;
                case 'user': // PG only
                    $this->user = $paramValue;
                    break;
                case 'password': // PG only
                    $this->password = $paramValue;
                    break;
                case 'sslmode': // PG only
                    $this->sslmode = $paramValue;
                    break;
                case 'sslcert': // PG only
                    $this->sslcert = $paramValue;
                    break;
                case 'sslkey': // PG only
                    $this->sslkey = $paramValue;
                    break;
                case 'sslrootcert': // PG only
                    $this->sslrootcert = $paramValue;
                    break;

The .env file looks something like this:

CRAFT_ENVIRONMENT=production
LANG=C.UTF-8
REDIS_HOST=******
DB_DRIVER=pgsql
DB_DSN=pgsql:host=******;port=5432;dbname=******
DB_SERVER=******
DB_DATABASE=******
DB_PASSWORD=******
DB_SCHEMA=public
DB_PORT=5432
DB_USER=******
ENVIRONMENT=production

items.title: Member/ User portal integrate with Dynamic 365
items.body:
I am looking for a solution to integrate my Dynamic 365 Database with CraftCMS Front-end user.

I have done some digging for how Dynamic 365 can integrate, but seems like it is limited at form (https://verbb.io/craft-plugins/formie/docs/integrations/crm) but not actually logging in into the CraftCMS member portal. I would like let user to store something like favorite post or link to the account but under 365 user database.

Does anyone have any thoughts or plugin to do these?

items.title: Imagine\Exception\RuntimeException Save operation failed Caused by ImagickException attempt to perform an operation not allowed by the security policy
items.body:
I have taken over a Craft 3 project and I'm having trouble opening a page of the site on DDEV (local dev), where I get the following error:

ImagickException: attempt to perform an operation not allowed by the security policy `@webRootBaseUrl/imager/assets/products/flat-equalizers-stabilize-wobbly-tables/Flat-equalizers_9ca8ba0c2061f2ff3f48a389965823fa.jpg' @ error/blob.c/OpenBlob/2671 in /var/www/html/vendor/pixelandtonic/imagine/src/Imagick/Image.php:389
Stack trace:
#0 /var/www/html/vendor/pixelandtonic/imagine/src/Imagick/Image.php(389): Imagick->writeImages('@webRootBaseUrl...', true)
#1 /var/www/html/vendor/spacecatninja/imager-x/src/transformers/CraftTransformer.php(297): Imagine\Imagick\Image->save('@webRootBaseUrl...', Array)
#2 /var/www/html/vendor/spacecatninja/imager-x/src/transformers/CraftTransformer.php(96): spacecatninja\imagerx\transformers\CraftTransformer->getTransformedImage(Object(spacecatninja\imagerx\models\LocalSourceImageModel), Array)
#3 /var/www/html/vendor/spacecatninja/imager-x/src/services/ImagerService.php(472): spacecatninja\imagerx\transformers\CraftTransformer->transform('/assets/product...', Array)

Every stackoverflow thread I can find with related Imagick issues reported (security policy), mentions the policy.xml file. However, a search of the Craft project repo (including vendor dir) results in no mention of policy.xml

Running Craft CMS 3.7.57 on DDEV

Thanks in advance.

items.title: How to reuse entries but change data in one field | Craft 5
items.body:
Let's say I have two Entry channels: Items and Collections. Collections have a relatedItems field in them. Items aren't unique to a Collection, and based on the Collection they're in, they could have a different quantity than the same item in a different collection.

I know I could create a matrix field called relatedItems, where each block has a field to choose the Item Entry and a number field to enter the quantity. My issue with this is there'll be thousands of Items and Collections, so there'll be multiple single-use Entry matrix blocks for the same Item spread out over many Collections. It feels incorrect, like I'll be generating unnecessary bloat in my database.

Another idea I thought of is to use a Table field in Collection entries. One column would be the related Item's Entry ID, and the other column would be the Item's quantity for that Collection. Now, there's only one Entry per Item (in the "Items" channel), whose ID can be referenced in multiple places along with a quantity. My only issue with this is that, when I view a Collection entry, I'll just see a bunch of IDs listed in a table. I can't easily jump to the Item entry to edit something like I could if it was a matrix block with a relation field.

Is there something I'm overlooking in Craft 5 to solve this? Since a channel has Entry elements and matrix fields (as of Craft 5) uses Entry elements too for their blocks, I don't want to create multiple entries/blocks for one Item. It seems redundant and excessive. Any advanced techniques I'm not thinking of?

PS, this isn't an e-commerce site. I'm trying to simplify the names of the channels for the sake of this post.

items.title: MatrixBlock::$deletedWithOwner must be ?bool
items.body:
I'm currently upgrading a Craft CMS project from version 4 to 5. The update works perfectly on my local environment, but when building on the staging server, I run into this error after running:

php8.2-sp craft clear-caches/all Error: Type of craft\elements\MatrixBlock::$deletedWithOwner must be ?bool (as in class craft\base\Element)

To troubleshoot, I have already:

Imported my production database both locally and on staging to rule out mismatches.

Used the craft super-table/migrate command and removed the Super Table plugin.

Despite these steps, the issue persists. Has anyone encountered this before, or does anyone have insights into what might be causing it?

Thanks in advance!

items.title: How do I make a link clear a sprig variable and trigger a reload?
items.body:
I'm using Sprig to filter a set of results. The filter values are shown as 'chips' which - when clicked on - remove that filter selection.

I know I can re-render the component with a simple sprig attribute:

<a href="#" sprig title="Remove this filter">fiction</a>

But how do I get it to un-set the filter too? I've tried attaching a click event to the link and clearing the form value, but it's not working.

items.title: Database Connection Error after updating from Craft 2 to 3
items.body:
Good evening all,

I am updating a small site from Craft 2 to 3, and after following the instructions in the documents, I loaded up the backend of my site and was greeted with the install screen.

I know that this typically means that the DB_TABLE_PREFIX is improperly set, but I triple-checked and I'm 99% certain that it's correct. I double-checked with my Craft 2, which looks like

// The prefix to use when naming tables. This can be no more than 5 characters.
    'tablePrefix' => 'craft',

And matched it to my .env file:

# The prefix that should be added to generated table names (only necessary if multiple things are sharing the same database)
DB_TABLE_PREFIX=craft

I also checked for a craft_info table, and that is present. The version listed in the version column is 2.9.2, if that makes a difference.

I am unable to login to my admin panel, or view my website now. I get the following when viewing my website:

Database Connection Error – craft\errors\DbConnectException
Craft CMS can’t connect to the database with the credentials in config/db.php.
↵
Caused by: Database Exception – yii\db\Exception

Any help is greatly appreciated!

-Shad

items.title: EVENT_REGISTER_SITE_URL_RULES not working
items.body:
I have created a custom module and registered a route like this.

EVENT::on(
            UrlManager::class,
            UrlManager::EVENT_REGISTER_SITE_URL_RULES,
            function (RegisterUrlRulesEvent $event) {
                // Create mailchimp subscription
                $event->rules['api/newsletter/subscribe'] = 'commerce-extension/mailchimp/subscribe';
            }
        );

However when I use this in my form it always returns a 404.

          <form method="post" accept-charset="UTF-8" novalidate>
            {{ csrfInput() }}
            {{ actionInput('/api/newsletter/subscribe') }}
            <input type="email" name="email">
            <button type="submit">Inschrijven</button>
          </form>

Using an actionInput that links directly to the controller works without issues.

{{ actionInput('/commerce-extension/mailchimp/subscribe') }}

Am I doing something wrong?

EDIT: I just noticed that the newly registered URL works when posting to it with fetch in javascript. Maybe there is something wrong with the actionInput?

items.title: craft multisite not working(only default site works) on server but completely works locally
items.body: i am encountering an issue with my multilingual craft website. Locally, everything works just fine, but when i upload it to the server, i get an error page not found for every site that is not my primary website. Any idea why this is happening? Also, i am not working with different folders for every language, all my templates just have an unique version for every language. Could anyone help me?

No new migrations found. Your system is up-to-date.

The SQL being executed was: ALTER TABLE craft_sections ADD minAuthors smallint(6) UNSIGNED NOT NULL DEFAULT 1 AFTER enableVersioning

items.title: How to remove links to an entry on scheduled date
items.body:
I have an entry that I need to come down on a specific date along with the button that leads to this entry from our homepage (it's an offer that expires at midnight).

If I disable the entry, links to the entry come down automatically, which is awesome. But my understanding is that I can't schedule the disable toggle and I need to use the Expiration Date instead. Expiring an entry does not seem to automatically remove links to it however.

If I could schedule an update to the homepage, I could have that button removed at the same time. But I don't see any way to schedule the applying of a draft to an entry that is already live.

I am an end user, not a developer. Am I missing something?

items.title: CP / Backend: Restrict single entry only to admins (edit / delete)
items.body:
I'm searching for an idea, to protect a single entry from being edited / deleted by editors (CP / backend). Of course, it's easy to do for fields or sections, but for a single entry ... hmmm ...

  • https://plugins.craftcms.com/isolate?craft4 -> that's not fitting to my needs
  • a concept could be, to manipulate the CP with CSS / JS -> looks like a dirty hack
  • maybe a custom plugin is needed -> a problem for me, unfortunalety

Any ideas? :)

items.title: how do I correctly configure createElementSelectorModal in Craft 5?
items.body:
I'm trying to get my modal to only display the entries from a single section, but I cannot find anything on the correct syntax to pass into the sources for the modal in Craft 5, and it doesn't appear as though older responses are relevant anymore.

document.getElementById('select-target-btn').addEventListener('click', function() {
    Craft.createElementSelectorModal('craft\\elements\\Entry', {
        multiSelect: false,
        sources: ['section:1'],
        criteria: {
            status: null,
        },
        onSelect: function(elements) {
            if (elements.length) {
                // Update target entry display
                const element = elements[0];
                const container = document.querySelector('[data-id]');
                container.setAttribute('data-id', element.id);
                container.querySelector('input[name="targetEntryId"]').value = element.id;
                container.querySelector('.title').textContent = element.label;
            }
        }
    });
});

As you can see, my sources, as per older documention, should be only displaying entries contained in the section with the ID of 1, but this doesn't display anything. If I replace ['section:1'] with '*' it will display all entries, with a sidebar of all sections.

What should I be passing into the sources parameter to only display entries from section 1?

I am running Craft CMS 5.7.11

Thanks

items.title: Updating/replacing query string parameters with twig
items.body:
I have code similar to below that basically just outputs links with query parameters:

{% for country in craft.categories.group("country").all() %}
   <a href="{{ url('wine') }}?country={{ country.id }}">{{ country.title }}</a>
{% endfor %}

{% for varietal in craft.categories.group("varietal").all() %}
   <a href="{{ url('wine') }}?varietal={{ varietal.id }}">{{ varietal.title }}</a>
{% endfor %}

{% for vintage in craft.categories.group("vintage").all() %}
   <a href="{{ url('wine') }}?vintage={{ vintage.id }}">{{ vintage.title }}</a>
{% endfor %}

This is all fine. Clicking on a link gives me what I need. For instance, clicking on a country link, I get this URL: site.com/wine?country=1

Here's where I'm stuck: how do I go about adding to the query string when clicking on multiple links not in the same category group. In other words if I click on a country link and a varietal link right after, I want the URL to be updated to look like so: site.com/wine?country=1&varietal=2

Then, if I click on a third link, such as vintage, update the URL like so: site.com/wine?country=1&varietal=2&vintage=3

But then, if I click on, say a different vintage link, just update the value rather than adding to the string further, like so: site.com/wine?country=1&varietal=2&vintage=99

items.title: Feed Me import into Matrix field stopped working a few months ago
items.body: Craft 3, Feed Me 4. A Feed Me import with dates going into a Matrix field used to work fine. As far as I know there were no code or JSON changes, and a couple of months ago (may or may not have been the same time as a plugin update) the Matrix fields are no longer being populated. The example data in the Feed Me config is fine, as per the picture, and if I assign the data to a regular field that field is populated. The JSON import is fine. Any ideas on where to look?

items.title: Error upgrading from CP
items.body:
I am working on a craft 4 site hosted on ServD. I use their plug in as recommended. My Craft license is also expired for updates. To keep their plugged in up-to-date I thought I'd get update just the plug plug in.

That turned out to be a mistake. During the update I got an error message that said 'Unknown error', and nothing anymore. It was compiler error so that the Craft run time and admin fails to run. Here is the complier error:

Type of craft\log\MonologTarget::$logger must be ?Psr\Log\LoggerInterface (as in class samdark\log\PsrTarget)

I have staging and production servers that are running fine. I have tried reverting my git commits on my dev server to match them. But that doesn't seem to help. The core code has become corrupted in someway, and Craft can't run anything.

Does anyone have suggestions on how to get it back in sync? I would be very appreciative!

No entryTypeHandle elements found.

I also tried using the entry type ID as well as section handles.

Do I misunderstand the purpose of this command or am I using it wrongly?

items.title: Redirect control panel access via .htaccess
items.body:
I have a website that is hosted on a subdomain, let's say: https://subdomain.maindomain.nl.

My craft content is in the maindomain/public_html/subdomain/git-module To make it work on the subdomain I added an htaccess file in the maindomain/public_html/subdomain with the following content:

RewriteCond %{HTTP_HOST} ^maindomain.subdomain.nl$ [NC,OR]
RewriteCond %{HTTP_HOST} ^www.subdomain.maindomain.nl$
RewriteCond %{REQUEST_URI} !git-module/web/
RewriteRule (.*) git-module/web/$1 [L]

For the front-end it is working. You can see everything on the https://subdomain.maindomain.nl url, but for the dashboard (which in this case is used for users as well, it still shows the whole url including /git-module/web like https://subdomain.maindomain.nl/git-module/web/admin.

My question is how I can delete this /git-module/web part of the url.

items.title: Factors that influence rendering time and memory consumption
items.body:
We are currently trying to improve one of our project's performance and therefor I try to understand factors that influence a page's rendering time and memory consumption.

Before optimizing complex pages with element queries I want to optimize a minimalistic twig template without any element query. This is the template:

<html>
<head>
    <title>Testing</title>
</head>
<body>
<h1>Testing</h1>

{% if craft.app.config.general.devMode %}
    <p>Dev Mode is <strong>enabled</strong>.</p>
{% else %}
    <p>Dev Mode is <strong>disabled</strong>.</p>
{% endif %}

{% if craft.app.config.general.enableTemplateCaching %}
    <p>Template Caching is <strong>enabled</strong>.</p>
{% else %}
    <p>Template Caching is <strong>disabled</strong>.</p>
{% endif %}
</body>
</html>

I put the template into the templates/ folder and call it directly.

I tried this on 3 different projects in production and enabled the frontend debug toolbar for my user. All 3 projects have almost the same setup: PHP Version (8.2), Craft Version (4.15.0.2), same machine, there are only differences in plugins, sections/entrytypes/entries and some minor module differences.

Interestingly I received 3 different results for the 3 projects:

  • 50ms rendering time and 1,5MB mem consumption
  • 65ms and 5MB
  • 180ms and 20MB

Next I cloned the slowest project to a second instance on the same machine and:

  • one after the other disabled all plugins: (almost) no change in rendering time and memory
  • removed all modules: no effects
  • removed almost all elements using the console command elements/delete-all-of-type: nothing
  • cleared all caches: nothing
  • did garbage collections: nothing

To be honest: I do not understand it. It's like Craft ignores my wishes to get a better understanding:

Do you have any other ideas? Or give me a hint on what is actually influencing rendering time and memory consumption?

items.title: Is it possible to change the order of entries or posts on the homepage?
items.body: I need to change the order of the posts / entries on our homepage - is there a way of doing that?

items.title: Need Swift Mailer to send emails as UTF-8
items.body:
I have a Craft 3 site that used Freeform Pro for a contact form. All submissions from this form need to be sent, via email to a third party vendor who parses the information and submits it to a CRM. The format for the emails they receive is very specific: plain-text, utf-8 character set.

When I send a test email from the contact form, it appears to default to ascii if there isn't a utf-8 character in the message. Is there a way to have SwiftMailer to send all emails as UTF-8?

items.title: Using UrlHelper from template or remove query string params when paginating?
items.body:
I have an implementation of a pagination which uses fetch() to get a subset of the page (i.e the page listing and pagination links), so when switching pages it gets the new list, as well as the pagination to update the available page links etc.

Due to CDN circumstances, I need to trigger this "partial" rendering via a querystring to allow caching this response independently from a full page render (vary by querystring) since the CDN doesn't care about headers for the cache key.

Now, the problem is, that any query string parameters that are passed in the request, will also be added the pagination urls (which, in general is fine, and wanted if those control additional filtering), but I want to exclude one parameter that is only used for triggering the partial rendering, e.g fetch=1.

However, the paginate function doesn't allow this, and I wanted to use the UrlHelper and the removeParam() on the paginate URLs, but I can't figure out how I can access that helper from the view?

To clarify, this is the flow:

page is /news

Pagination urls are: [/news] [/news/p2] [/news/p3]

When clicking on [/news/p2], I will append a query string parameter called fetch=1 that returns partial content and new pagination:

page is now /news/p1 (via history.pushState), but fetched /news/p1?fetch=1

Pagination urls are: [/news?fetch=1] [/news/p2?fetch=1] [/news/p3?fetch=1]

I want the urls to be correct, to allow a correct fallback linking (non-fetch).

I can of course do some regex magic on the pagination urls in the template to overcome this, but having correct URL aware parsing would be much cleaner. Having a filter that could parse and remove query string parameters would also be great, like:

{{ pagination.nextUrl|removeParam('fetch') }}

But I can't find any such builtin filter.

items.title: send e-mails via smtp without authentification
items.body:
I try to send e-mails via SMTP without authentification and I'm getting the following error:

There was an error testing your email settings.

I checked my setup on https://www.smtper.net/. No problems!

I read through the troubleshooting-email-errors page but still found no solution for my problem.

Why can't I find a log under craft/storage/runtime/logs/craft.log. This file does not exist on my system. There is only craft/storage/logs/web.log.

When I use another SMTP server (with auth) everything works as expected. Any ideas on how to find out what's going on here?

items.title: Lightswitch field no longer saving
items.body:
We have a nag screen to remind users to fill out their public profile. The nag has an ignore option (as seen below) that updates a field on the user's record called ignoreProfilePrompt. The form is triggered by the onlick event of the "Don't ask me again" link.

Before nagging, I check the value of currentUser.ignoreProfilePrompt.

This has worked well for a few years now, or at least was working the last time it was tested. Recently during testing I'm seeing that the form that updates the ignoreProfilePrompt field is no longer updating the field on the user's record.

<p class="text-muted">It looks as though you don't have much information in your account profile. Completing your profile will put your skills on the map at let others contact you.</p>

            <form id="ignoreForm" class="border-0" method="POST" enctype="multipart/form-data">
                {{ csrfInput() }}
                {{ actionInput('users/save-user') }}
                {{ hiddenInput('userId', currentUser.id) }}
                {{ hiddenInput('fields[ignoreProfilePrompt]', true) }} 
            </form>
            <p class="mb-2 text-left"> <a class="btn btn-primary" href="/user/editProfile">Edit your profile</a> <a class="btn" href="#" type="submit" onclick="$('#ignoreForm').submit();">Don't ask me again</a></p>

In the log file is this:

$_POST = [
'CRAFT_CSRF_TOKEN' => '••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••'
'action' => 'users/save-user'
'userId' => '48418'
'fields' => [
    'ignoreProfilePrompt' => '1'
]

]

Checking the user's record in the backend I see that the lightswitch is false. See anything I'm missing?

This is Craft CMS Pro 4.14.7

items.title: Express Forms Not Sending Notification Emails
items.body:
Craft 3.3.0.1 Express Forms 1.0.4

I have an Express Forms set up. It submits and saves entries to the DB which can be viewed in the CP, however email notifications are not sent.

Sending a test email via settings > email works as expected as does a different Express Forms form, but this one does not and I am flummoxed.

when I check the Express forms log I see this:

Here is a simplified template of my form where you can see the notification email address are set to a field in the page entry or if that is empty default email addresses are used.

{# 
// set send email address from careers entry 
#}
{% set sendEmailAddress %}
  {# loop through emails from job entry page and set the form to send to those emails #}
  {% for email in entry.applicationEmails %}
    "{{ email.emailAddress }}"{% if not loop.last %},{% endif %}
  {% else %}
  {# if empty send to this address #}
    "test@domain.com", "test2@domain.com"
  {% endfor %}
{% endset %}

{# this testing div outputs emails as expected #}
<div class="testing">
  {{ sendEmailAddress }}
</div><!-- /.testing -->


{% set form = craft.expressforms.form("jobapplication") %}


{# General Error Handling #}
{% if not form.valid %}
    <div class="alert alert-danger" role="alert">
        {{ "Error! Please review the form and try submitting again."|t }}
        {% if form.errors|length %}
            <ul>
                {% for error in form.errors %}
                    <li>{{ error|t }}</li>
                {% endfor %}
            </ul>
        {% endif %}
    </div>
{% endif %}


{{ form.openTag({
   return: '?success=1',
   dynamicNotifications: {
      to: [sendEmailAddress],
      template: "job-application.twig",
   }
}) }}

  {# 
    Various Fields here
  #}

  <button type="submit" data-application-submit class="btn btn-default" disabled>{{ "contact_submit"|t }}</button>

{{ form.closeTag }}

I've tried various approaches to using dynamic emails notifications but nothing seems to work.

My email notification template looks like this where {eMail} is the email field name in the form.

---
name: 'Job Application Email Notification Template'
description: 'submits job applications to reciever'
fromName: 'Strongco'
fromEmail: '$SYSTEM_EMAIL'
replyTo: '{eMail}'
cc: null
bcc: null
subject: 'Job Application: {positionAppliedFor}'
includeAttachments: true
---
<p>The following submission came in on {{ dateCreated|date('l, F j, Y \\a\\t g:ia') }}.</p>

<ul>
    {% for field in form.fields %}
        <li><strong>{{ field.label }}:</strong> {{ field.valueAsString }}</li>
    {% endfor %}
</ul>

Please advise.

items.title: Internal Server Error on Save / Dashboard
items.body:
A client's site is producing Internal Server Errors randomly in the Craft backend (3.7.53.1). The Dashboard produces an error. Saving certain entries produces an error (especially when enabling entires.

I have done the following:

  • Increased Memory Limits to 512M
  • Checked PHP Errors (nothing)
  • Checked web.log. It's very hard to make sense of this. There are no clear error messages here
  • Checked System Report (nothing obvious).

Can anyone advise me on how to manage this?

items.title: Excluding entries with a certain category from a query
items.body:
I'm trying to build a search page on a Craft 3.x site that returns results based on values in the query string. That part I've got working OK with the code below:

{% if craft.app.request.getQueryString()|length %}

  {# Product Categories #}
  {% set productCategories = craft.app.request.getParam('category') %}
  {% set productCatsIds = [] %}
  {% if productCategories %}
    {# work out the categories from the querystring #}
  {% endif %}

  {% set tags = craft.app.request.getParam('tags') %}
  {% if tags %}
      {# similar to above #}
  {% endif %}

  {% set relations = [] %}

  {# Product Categories relation (OR within) #}
  {% if productCatsIds|length %}
    {% set relations = relations|merge([{
      targetElement: productCatsIds,
      field: 'categories'
    }]) %}
  {% endif %}

  {# Tags relation (OR within) #}
  {% if tagIds|length %}
    {% set relations = relations|merge([{
      targetElement: tagIds
    }]) %}
  {% endif %}

  {% set query = craft.entries()
    .section('products')
    .with([
      'categories',
      'images'
    ])
  %}

  {% if relations|length %}
    {% set andConditions = ['and'] %}
    {% for relation in relations %}
        {% set andConditions = andConditions|merge([relation]) %}
    {% endfor %}
    {% set query = query.relatedTo(andConditions) %}
  {% endif %}

{% endif %}

{% paginate query.limit(10).orderBy('title') as pageInfo, products %}

But I also need to be able to remove entries from a certain category from the results.

I thought I could do it by adding this:

{% set cat = craft.categories({slug: 'remove'}).all() %}
{% set ids = craft.entries().relatedTo(cat).ids() %}
{% set query = query.id(ids ? ['not']|merge(ids) : null) %}

but that returns an incorrect number of results and also still returns some results with the category I want removed.

How should this work?

Edit to include something that seems like it should work but doesn't…

I've tried adding this at the end of all the other queries but before the paginate tag:

{% set cat = craft.categories().slug('remove').one() %}
{% if cat %}
    {% set excludedIds = cat.descendants().ids()|merge([cat.id]) %}
    {% set query = query.relatedTo({
        targetElement: excludedIds,
        field: 'categories',
        invert: true
    }) %}
{% endif %}

but it produces the wrong number of results (far fewer than there should be) and also still displays results with the category I want removed.

Edit 2: Also, tried this to merge the exclusion with previous relations:

{% set relations = relations|merge([{
    targetElement: excludedIds,
    field: 'categories',
    invert: true
}]) %}

items.title: Entries Live Preview Only Reflects Changes After 'Save' and Not On Drafts
items.body:
I just started using CraftCMS Pro 5.4.6 without access to the backend (only Control Panel), so did my limited research to the best of my ability.

When I'm creating entries, the 'Preview' button successfully pulls up the edit panel and website side by side, but will not reflect any changes until I hit 'Save'. Some changes I've tried: updating texts, disabling/enabling content blocks, inserting images, etc. This is not the expected behavior of the 'Preview' button from what I've read (it should be automatically saving changes to a draft which appears in the preview panel). I see the page reload when I make changes, but no changes are reflected visually.

I've also tried using the preview panel on a draft, where no changes are reflected in the Preview panel even after I hit save.

I tried looking at Chrome Inspector for any potential errors, and with my limited understanding of API calls, nothing stood out as a failed POST/GET.

Would appreciate any advice, context, etc! Thank you :)

items.title: Craft 3 - Moving assets between folders causes apache connection to max out
items.body:
In Craft 3, I keep running into an issue when I try to move a large amount of files (around 200) by dragging and dropping from my “Holding” folder into a “Live” folder using Craft Assets section. Both folders are hosted on Amazon S3. Even moving three files seems to take a really long time (about a minute).

After checking the server logs it looks like I am filling my max apache connection due to the search-index-perform-action. The error suggested I increase my “MaxRequestWorkers” setting.

I move about 2,000 files every month and never had a problem on Craft 2. I don’t know if should change the “MaxRequestWorkers” setting or if there is something else I should consider doing. If I should change the setting, where is it, I can't seem to find it.

I use Transmit FTP to initially move the files into the Holding folder and then index the assets in that folder. Then in Craft CMS control panel, I drag the files from Holding folder to the main Live folder so that the asset index gets updated.

UPDATE

I looked in my phperror.log file and found these three types of errors occuring:

PHP Fatal error:  Allowed memory size of 268435456 bytes exhausted (tried to allocate 1048576 bytes) in /storage/avxxxxx/www/public_html/vendor/craftcms/cms/src/helpers/DateTimeHelper.php on line 231

PHP Fatal error:  Allowed memory size of 268435456 bytes exhausted (tried to allocate 20480 bytes) in /storage/avxxxxx/www/public_html/vendor/craftcms/cms/src/elements/db/ElementQuery.php on line 2215

PHP Fatal error:  Maximum execution time of 90 seconds exceeded in /storage/avxxxxx/www/public_html/vendor/league/flysystem/src/Util.php on line 322

PHP Fatal error:  Maximum execution time of 90 seconds exceeded in /storage/avxxxxx/www/public_html/vendor/league/flysystem-aws-s3-v3/src/AwsS3Adapter.php on line 294

PHP Fatal error:  Maximum execution time of 90 seconds exceeded in /storage/avxxxxx/www/public_html/vendor/league/flysystem/src/Util/ContentListingFormatter.php on line 52

It seems like when I move a file from Holding (950 files) to Live (20,000) files is where things get hung up.

  • Holding -> Live = 1 file takes 7 seconds to move
  • Live -> Holding = 1 file takes 20 seconds to move

Could it be an issue with the cache or search-index?

Here are my current settings if it helps:

memory_limit 256M

max_execution_time = 90

Max Requests = Per Child: 300 - Keep Alive: on - Max Per Connection: 150

Timeouts = Connection: 10 - Keep-Alive: 2

items.title: Updates always error out
items.body:
Every time I try to run an update, I get these errors. Installing plugins won't work either. I'm using Media Temple / Grid server hosting. I've increased the file size limits in php.ini as well. But I'm at a loss.

Status: Internal Server Error

Response: 

PHP Fatal Error 'yii\base\ErrorException' with message 'Out of memory (allocated 226500608) (tried to allocate 4096 bytes)'
in /nfs/c07/h01/mnt/179491/domains/bluepearl.villas/vendor/composer/composer/src/Composer/DependencyResolver/RuleWatchGraph.php:52
Stack trace:
#0 [internal function]: yii\base\ErrorHandler->handleFatalError()
#1 {main}

items.title: Custom field value as toEmail with CraftCMS Contact Form
items.body:
Is it possible to set a recipients email address via a custom field with the Contact Form plugin?

I have tried using {% set recipient = entry.recipientEmail %}

And then hashing the recipient variable like:

<input type="hidden" name="toEmail" value="{{ recipient|hash }}">

But it doesn't work.

items.title: Cannot get Craft 5.x Matrix entry relatedTo parent entry
items.body:
I have:

  • a section called events
  • with a Matrix field called programme
  • which has several entry types, of which one is progContent.

I want to be able to query the progContent entries based on their relationship to the parent events entry of which they are a part (and some other criteria I’m leaving out).

I have an events entry element saved into a variable called evEntry, and that entry has, for now, a single progContent entry saved into its programme field.

Unsurprisingly, I can get the progContent entry with:

{% set entry = craft.entries({ type: 'progContent' }).one() ?? null %}

But when I try to narrow results down based on the relationship (as I will need to do with more content), the following returns null:

{% set entry = craft.entries({
    type: 'progContent',
    relatedTo: evEntry
}).one() ?? null %}

I’ve tried the following to be more precise:

{% set entry = craft.entries({
    type: 'progContent',
    relatedTo: { 
        targetElement: evEntry,
        field: 'programme'
    }
}).one() ?? null %}

…and variants with sourceElement and evEntry.id but am still getting nowhere.

Where am I going wrong please?

items.title: Amazon S3/Cloudfront bucket providing "Unable to check if <img> exists" error when uploading an asset
items.body:
I have just set up my first S3/CloudFront service to handle assets and it all seems to be connected right, but when I upload an asset into the volume that is stored on S3, I get this odd error in CraftCMS which I haven't been able to find many resources about:

This doesn't appear to be a permissions/policy error as this was a separate error I had in the process of putting this together. I have tried to upload different images in the hope the one I was trying was corrupt, but it appears to be happening with all.

items.title: Within the CP, how can I filter the listed Entries based on a Lightswitch field value?
items.body:
I tried these kind of searches in the CP search field (on top of the Entry list):

  • fieldHandle:true / fieldHandle:false
  • fieldHandle:1 / fieldHandle:0
  • fieldHandle:* / -fieldHandle:*
  • fieldHandle:not 1 / fieldHandle:'not 1'
  • etc.

Unfortunately, none of these returns the expected entries.

items.title: An unknown error has occurred in asset reindex
items.body:
I am getting the error: An unknown error occurred for each file in a folder when I try Update Asset Indexes. The folder is a local folder that is outside of webroot. The files just contain json data. I have looked through Craft log files and apache log files and cannot find anything. Any ideas?

I have tried the following from other questions with the same error message and none of these made an difference.

Deleted entire craft/storage/runtime/ folder

An unknown error occurred - no log entry

Made sure no record in table assetindex has null for dateModified column

"An unknown error occurred" in assets screen

Reset folder ownership

permissions userphotos "an unknown error occurred"

items.title: Preview in a Craft 5 multisite setup
items.body:
I am developing a multisite project in Craft 5. There are three different sites, each with their own unique domain. Each site uses the same templates, but each has its own unique content, e.g. they all have a 'products' section, but each has its own products.

I created unique product sections for each site and disabled the other sites in the Site Setting control. Also set the Propagation Method to '...only sites enabled'.

Everything is working fine. However, when I try to preview an entry the preview target always uses the default site url. My guess is that I need to set the Preview Target url to something unique for the site/section, e.g. using the environment variable $SITE_B, but have not been able to come up with anything that works. Any help would be greatly appreciated!

items.title: Upgrading to 3.7.26 causes tempnam() error from twig's FilesystemCache.php
items.body:
I am upgrading a Craft project that I have been neglecting for a little bit too long. I am upgrading to 3.7.26 from 3.4.25; on top of this, I am upgrading from PHP 7.1 to PHP 7.4.

I'm also updating a variety of plugins on top of this as well. Attached is my composer.json file below.

After nuking my vendor file, completing the php craft update all, and installing new required dependencies (multibyte string, ext-curl, etc), I'm getting the following error.

## PHP Notice – yii\base\ErrorException
tempnam(): file created in the system's temporary directory

line 64: $tmpFile = tempnam($dir, basename($key));

I've tried nuking my vendor folder again, trying in different browsers, updating composer, searching SO a bunch, and I'm not sure where to check next.

Any thoughts would be really helpful, thanks.

My composer.json file:

{

items.title: Craft CMS 5 Ddev local build suddenly giving 404 page
items.body:
I recently deployed a local Craft CMS install to our server and took several steps to prepare, including setting the site offline using an offline.php template and configuring a .env file to connect to the database. I also created a backup of the original configuration.

The deployment didnt work correctly so after the weekend, I restored the .env file to its original state, but now I’m encountering an HTTP 404 error on both the frontend and the backend of the site. I’m unable to access either side of the site. I'm not sure if setting it offline could have anything to do with this issue.

Is it possible that I accidentally broke something else during this process? What steps can I take to resolve this and restore access to both the frontend and backend?

Thank you for any help!

items.title: Pass Variable back from Partial
items.body:
Whilst passing a variable to a partial is very useful, I need to be able to set a variable inside the partial that can be read by another partial.

Specifically, I have a partial which sets the layout to be either full width or normal (constrained) width:

  1. If full width is chosen, it's just a case of closing divs.
  2. Choosing normal reinstates the closed divs.

I want to idiot proof it so the client couldn't set normal when it's already normal. That could get very ugly.

So my strategy is to set a variable that knows whether it is full width or normal width. It's initially set to normal width. The problem comes when changing the status of the variable within the partial. Next time the layout width partial is selected, it thinks it's always normal.
